[59] Delano acknowledges that he is knowingly and voluntarily waiving and releasing any and all rights or claims he may have under the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ("ADEA"), as amended by the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act ("OWBPA"), 29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq. Delano further acknowledges and agrees as follows:
[60] (a) Delano has read this Agreement in its entirety, understands each of its terms and provisions, and agrees to be bound hereby.
[61] (b) The consideration provided to Delano under Section 3 of this Agreement exceeds anything of value to which Delano is already entitled and constitutes adequate and sufficient consideration for the release of ADEA claims contained herein.
[62] (c) Delano has been given a period of fourteen (14) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement within which to consider whether to execute it, which Delano acknowledges is a sufficient and reasonable period of time.
[63] (d) Delano is entering into this Agreement of his own free will and volition, without coercion, duress, threat, or undue pressure from any person or entity, and with full knowledge of its terms, effects, and consequences.
[64] (e) Delano acknowledges that this Agreement is written in a manner calculated to be understood by him and that he, in fact, understands the terms, conditions, and effect of this Agreement.
[65] (f) Delano has not been coerced, threatened, or otherwise pressured into signing this Agreement, and Delano's decision to execute this Agreement has been made freely, knowingly, and voluntarily.
[66] 
[67] Section 6. Confidentiality
[68] Delano agrees that the terms, amount, and existence of this Agreement, including the Settlement Payment and each of its component parts, shall be kept strictly confidential. Delano shall not disclose, publish, or communicate any information regarding this Agreement, its terms, its financial components, or the negotiations leading to its execution to any person or entity, except as follows:
[69] (i) to Delano's spouse or domestic partner, provided that such individual agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the information;
[70] (ii) to Delano's legal counsel at Engel & Associates, P.C., or any successor legal counsel retained by Delano;
[71] (iii) to Delano's tax advisor, accountant, or financial advisor, solely for the purpose of obtaining tax, accounting, or financial advice, provided that such advisor agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the information; or
[72] (iv) as required by applicable law, regulation, court order, subpoena, or other compulsory legal process.
[73] In the event that Delano is compelled by legal process to disclose any information subject to this confidentiality provision, Delano shall provide Greenleaf with prompt written notice of such compulsion so as to permit Greenleaf to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. In the event that Delano breaches this confidentiality provision, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and all other available legal and equitable remedies, including recovery of damages sustained as a result of such breach.
[74] Section 7. Non-Disparagement
[75] Neither Party shall, at any time after the Effective Date, make, publish, or communicate, or cause or encourage any other person or entity to make, publish, or communicate, any disparaging, defamatory, derogatory, or negative statements, whether written or oral, about the other Party, including but not limited to statements regarding the other Party's business practices, products, services, financial condition, management, employees, corporate governance, or the circumstances of Delano's employment with or separation from Greenleaf. For purposes of this Section 7, "Greenleaf" includes its officers, directors, and members of senior management, specifically including Sandra Wojcik (CEO), Tyler Huang (General Counsel), Patricia Engel (COO), and Leanne Foss (HR Director), each in their individual and official capacities. Any breach of this non-disparagement provision shall entitle the non-breaching Party to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies. The obligations set forth in this Section 7 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall continue in perpetuity.
[76] Section 8. Tax Provisions
[77] The Parties acknowledge and agree that the tax treatment of the Settlement Payment shall be as follows:
[78] (a) The compensatory damages payment of $275,000.00 described in Section 3(a) shall be treated as non-wage income, shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC issued to Delano, and shall not be subject to federal, state, or local payroll tax withholding. Delano shall be solely responsible for the timely payment of all federal, state, and local income taxes due on this amount.
[79] (b) The back pay payment of $125,000.00 described in Section 3(b) shall be treated as wage income, shall be subject to all applicable federal, state, and local payroll tax withholdings (including FICA, federal income tax, and Oregon state income tax), and shall be reported on IRS Form W-2 for the applicable tax year.
[80] (c) The attorney fees payment of $75,000.00 described in Section 3(c) shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC issued to Engel & Associates, P.C., at its federal Tax Identification Number.
[81] (d) The accelerated vesting of Restricted Stock Units described in Section 3(d) shall be treated in accordance with the terms of the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and applicable provisions of the Internal Revenue Code.
[82] Greenleaf makes no representations or warranties to Delano regarding the tax consequences of any payment or benefit provided under this Agreement, and Delano acknowledges that he has not relied upon any statement or representation by Greenleaf or its attorneys concerning the tax treatment of any portion of the Settlement Payment. Greenleaf shall have no obligation to indemnify, defend, or hold harmless Delano for any tax liability, including interest and penalties, arising from or related to the payments or benefits made under this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for its own tax obligations arising from the transactions contemplated by this Agreement.
[83] 
[84] Section 9. Non-Competition
[85] For a period of eighteen (18) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant, independent contractor, officer, director, partner, member, owner, investor (other than as a holder of less than two percent (2%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly traded company), agent, advisor, or in any other capacity, engage in, be employed by, consult for, or otherwise provide services to any competitor in the organic food industry within the states of Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and California.
[86] For purposes of this Section 9, a "competitor" shall mean any business, firm, company, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, or other entity that manufactures, distributes, markets, sells, or otherwise deals in organic food products, including but not limited to granola, trail mixes, dried fruit, frozen meals, plant-based snacks, organic beverages, or similar products.
[87] In the event that Delano breaches this non-competition provision, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and all other available legal and equitable remedies, including recovery of damages sustained as a result of such breach. In addition, the eighteen (18) month non-competition period shall be tolled during any period during which Delano is in breach of this provision, and shall resume upon the date on which such breach ceases.
[88] Section 10. Non-Solicitation
[89] For a period of eighteen (18) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether on his own behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity:
[90] (a) solicit, recruit, hire, engage, or encourage, or attempt to solicit, recruit, hire, engage, or encourage, any employee, independent contractor, or consultant of Greenleaf to leave the Company's employ or engagement, or to accept employment or engagement with any other person or entity;
[91] (b) solicit, divert, or take away, or attempt to solicit, divert, or take away, the business or patronage of any customer, supplier, vendor, distributor, or business partner of Greenleaf with whom Delano had material contact or about whom Delano obtained confidential information during the last two (2) years of his employment with Greenleaf.
[92] For the avoidance of doubt, this non-solicitation provision shall not prohibit Delano from making general solicitations of employment through public advertisements or employment postings not specifically directed at Greenleaf employees. In the event of a breach, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies.
[93] Section 11. Employment References
[94] Upon request from any prospective employer or other third party, Greenleaf agrees to provide a positive, neutral, or at minimum non-negative employment reference for Delano. Such reference shall confirm Delano's dates of employment (June 12, 2017, through March 14, 2025), his final title of Vice President of Supply Chain Operations, and shall include a statement that Delano was a valued member of the Greenleaf team who made significant contributions to the Company's supply chain operations during his tenure. All reference inquiries regarding Delano shall be directed to Leanne Foss, Human Resources Director, or her designee. Greenleaf shall instruct Sandra Wojcik, Tyler Huang, Patricia Engel, Leanne Foss, and all other current employees who may receive reference inquiries concerning Delano to refrain from providing any information beyond the reference described above and shall not, under any circumstances, provide any information suggesting that Delano was terminated for cause, for performance-related reasons, or in connection with any internal complaint or investigation. Greenleaf shall issue written instructions to the foregoing individuals within seven (7) calendar days of the Effective Date implementing the requirements of this Section 11.
[95] Section 12. Mutual Release by Greenleaf
[96] Greenleaf, on behalf of itself and each of the Releasees, hereby irrevocably and unconditionally releases, acquits, and forever discharges Delano from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits, damages, losses, expenses, and liabilities of every kind and nature, whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, that Greenleaf has, has ever had, or may hereafter have against Delano, arising out of, related to, or in any way connected with Delano's employment with Greenleaf or the termination thereof, from the beginning of time through the Effective Date, except for claims arising from: (i) Delano's fraud, embezzlement, or criminal conduct discovered after the Effective Date; (ii) Delano's breach of any provision of this Agreement; or (iii) any obligation of Delano that survives termination of employment under any prior written agreement between the Parties, to the extent that such obligation is not superseded by this Agreement.
[97] 
[98] Section 13. No Admission of Liability
[99] This Agreement is a compromise and settlement of disputed claims. Nothing contained in this Agreement, including the payment of the Settlement Payment, shall be construed as an admission of liability, wrongdoing, or violation of any federal, state, or local law, regulation, or ordinance by either Party. Greenleaf specifically denies that it engaged in any unlawful retaliation, age discrimination, or other wrongful or discriminatory conduct toward Delano or any other individual. Delano specifically denies any wrongdoing or misconduct in connection with his employment with Greenleaf. The Parties enter into this Agreement solely for the purpose of avoiding the costs, burdens, and uncertainties of litigation and to achieve a full and final resolution of all disputes between them.
[100] Section 14. Re-Employment Eligibility
[101] Delano shall not be barred from future employment with Greenleaf and shall be considered for open positions for which he is qualified, on the same basis as any other external applicant, without prejudice or adverse consideration arising from the circumstances of his separation from employment or the claims asserted in connection with this Agreement. Greenleaf shall not maintain any internal notation, flag, code, or designation in its human resources records, personnel files, applicant tracking systems, or any other employment-related database or record that would indicate Delano is ineligible for rehire or that would otherwise adversely affect Delano's candidacy for future employment with Greenleaf.
[102] Section 15. Representations and Warranties
[103] (a) Delano represents and warrants that:
[104] (i) He has not assigned, transferred, conveyed, or otherwise disposed of any claim, demand, right, or cause of action released herein to any person or entity;
[105] (ii) He has not filed any lawsuit, complaint, charge, or other proceeding against Greenleaf or any of the Releasees with any court, tribunal, or governmental agency, other than the Oregon OSHA complaint referenced in the Recitals above (Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872), and he agrees not to file any such lawsuit, complaint, charge, or proceeding in the future with respect to any claim released herein, except as may be required by law or to enforce the terms of this Agreement;
[106] (iii) He has the full legal capacity and authority to execute this Agreement and to perform his obligations hereunder, and his execution and performance of this Agreement does not conflict with or violate any other agreement to which he is a party.
[107] (b) Greenleaf represents and warrants that:
[108] (i) It has the full corporate power and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform its obligations hereunder, and the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action;
[109] (ii) The person executing this Agreement on behalf of Greenleaf is duly authorized to do so and to bind Greenleaf to the terms and conditions contained herein.
[110] Section 16. Execution and Consideration Period
[111] Delano shall have fourteen (14) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement to review, consider, and execute it. If Delano does not execute and return a signed copy of this Agreement to Engel & Associates, P.C., or to Greenleaf's General Counsel within the fourteen (14) calendar day period, this offer of settlement shall automatically expire and be of no further force or effect, and neither Party shall have any obligation hereunder.
[112] This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which, when taken together, shall constitute one and the same instrument. Facsimile signatures and electronic (PDF) signatures transmitted by email or other electronic means shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes of this Agreement and shall be sufficient to bind each Party hereto.
[113] 
[114] Section 17. Severability
[115] If any provision of this Agreement, or the application thereof to any person, entity, or circumstance, is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect as if such invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision had not been included herein. The Parties agree that in the event any provision is determined to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving the Parties' original intent to the greatest extent possible.
[116] Section 18. Entire Agreement
[117] This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, representations, agreements, promises, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter, including but not limited to any prior offer letters, employment agreements, memoranda of understanding, or term sheets exchanged between the Parties in connection with the resolution of the disputes addressed herein. No amendment, modification, supplement, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by both Parties. No waiver of any breach or default under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any subsequent breach or default.
[118] IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims as of the date first set forth above.

text: Let me extract clean pPr/rPr XML for a section header and a body paragraph to replicate styles.

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.arguments:
{"command": "cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && python3 -c "
import docx
from lxml import etree
import re
d = docx.Document('documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx')
W='{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main}'
def clean(el):
    s=etree.tostring(el).decode()
    s=re.sub(r'xmlns:[a-z0-9]+=\"[^\"]*\"','',s)
    s=re.sub(r' +',' ',s)
    s=s.replace(' >','>')
    return s
for idx in [84,85,30,31]:
    p=d.paragraphs[idx]
    ppr=p._p.find(f'{W}pPr')
    print(f'--- PARA {idx}: {p.text[:40]!r} ---')
    print('pPr:', clean(ppr) if ppr is not None else None)
    for j,r in enumerate(p.runs):
        rpr=r._r.find(f'{W}rPr')
        print(f'  run{j} rPr:', clean(rpr) if rpr is not None else None)
""}

tool_calls.arguments:
{"file_path": "/tmp/build_redline.py", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build a tracked-changes redline of the draft settlement agreement.

Preserves original formatting for unchanged paragraphs; for changed paragraphs,
keeps the bold lead-in label run and applies a word-level diff (diff_match_patch)
to the body, emitting native <w:ins>/<w:del> revision elements. New paragraphs
(new sections / carve-outs) are inserted as wholly-inserted paragraphs with an
inserted paragraph mark.
"""
import sys, zipfile, shutil
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree
from diff_match_patch import diff_match_patch

W = "http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main"
XML = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
NSMAP = {"w": W}
AUTHOR = "Greenleaf GC Review"
WHEN = "2025-05-16T00:00:00Z"

def q(tag): return f"{{{W}}}{tag}"

ORIG = Path("documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx")
WORK = Path("workdir_redline")
OUT = Path("output/settlement-agreement-markup.docx")

# ---------- style templates (clean, no namespace decls) ----------
HEADER_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:keepNext/><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="200" w:after="80"/><w:ind w:left="0"/></w:pPr>' % W
HEADER_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:b/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W
BODY_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="0" w:after="120"/><w:jc w:val="both"/></w:pPr>' % W
BODY_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W

class Rev:
    def __init__(self):
        self.n = 1000
    def next(self):
        self.n += 1
        return self.n
REV = Rev()

def parse_frag(xml):
    return etree.fromstring(xml)

def clone_rpr(rpr_el):
    if rpr_el is None:
        return parse_frag(BODY_RPR)
    return etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el))

def make_run(text, rpr_el):
    r = etree.SubElement(etree.Element(q("g")), q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return r

def make_ins(text, rpr_el):
    ins = etree.Element(q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr_el))
    return ins

def make_del(text, rpr_el):
    d = etree.Element(q("del"))
    d.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    d.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    d.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(d, q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("delText"))
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return d

def word_diff(old, new):
    dmp = diff_match_patch()
    diffs = dmp.diff_main(old, new)
    dmp.diff_cleanupSemantic(diffs)
    out = []
    for op, txt in diffs:
        if op == 0: out.append(("eq", txt))
        elif op == 1: out.append(("ins", txt))
        else: out.append(("del", txt))
    return out

def is_bold_run(r):
    rpr = r.find(q("rPr"))
    return rpr is not None and rpr.find(q("b")) is not None

def run_text(r):
    return "".join(t.text or "" for t in r.findall(q("t")))

def rebuild_paragraph(p_el, new_full_text):
    """Rebuild runs of p_el to turn old text -> new_full_text with tracked changes."""
    runs = p_el.findall(q("r"))
    old_full = "".join(run_text(r) for r in runs)
    rpr_body = clone_rpr(runs[-1].find(q("rPr")) if runs else None)
    # remove all existing runs
    for r in runs:
        p_el.remove(r)
    # Case: bold lead-in label run that is unchanged prefix of new text
    if runs and is_bold_run(runs[0]):
        label = run_text(runs[0])
        if new_full_text.startswith(label):
            # keep the bold label run as-is
            p_el.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(runs[0])))
            old_body = old_full[len(label):]
            new_body = new_full_text[len(label):]
            for op, txt in word_diff(old_body, new_body):
                if not txt: continue
                if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))
            return
    # Default: full-paragraph diff
    for op, txt in word_diff(old_full, new_full_text):
        if not txt: continue
        if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))

def make_inserted_paragraph(text, style):
    ppr_xml = HEADER_PPR if style == "header" else BODY_PPR
    rpr_xml = HEADER_RPR if style == "header" else BODY_RPR
    p = etree.Element(q("p"))
    ppr = etree.fromstring(ppr_xml)
    # inserted paragraph mark
    rpr_in_ppr = etree.SubElement(ppr, q("rPr"))
    ins_mark = etree.SubElement(rpr_in_ppr, q("ins"))
    ins_mark.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    ins_mark.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    ins_mark.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    p.append(ppr)
    ins = etree.SubElement(p, q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(ins, q("r"))
    r.append(etree.fromstring(rpr_xml))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return p

# ================= REVISIONS (paragraph index -> new full text) =================

# ================= BUILD =================
if WORK.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(WORK)
WORK.mkdir(parents=True)
with zipfile.ZipFile(ORIG) as z:
    z.extractall(WORK)

doc_xml = WORK / "word" / "document.xml"
tree = etree.parse(str(doc_xml))
root = tree.getroot()
body = root.find(q("body"))

# Collect <w:p> elements in document order (exclude sectPr)
paras = [c for c in body if c.tag == q("p")]
print(f"Found {len(paras)} paragraphs")

# Apply revisions
for idx, new_text in revisions.items():
    p_el = paras[idx]
    old_text = "".join(run_text(r) for r in p_el.findall(q("r")))
    rebuild_paragraph(p_el, new_text)
    new_now = "".join(run_text(r) for r in p_el.findall(q("r")) if r.getparent().tag != q("del"))
    # sanity: the eq+ins text should equal new_text
    print(f"  revised para {idx}: old_len={len(old_text)} new_len={len(new_text)}")

# Apply insertions (process in reverse anchor order so earlier insertions don't shift later refs)
for anchor_idx in sorted(insertions.keys(), reverse=True):
    anchor = paras[anchor_idx]
    for (text, style) in reversed(insertions[anchor_idx]):
        new_p = make_inserted_paragraph(text, style)
        anchor.addnext(new_p)
    print(f"  inserted {len(insertions[anchor_idx])} para(s) after {anchor_idx}")

tree.write(str(doc_xml), xml_declaration=True, encoding="UTF-8", standalone=True)

# Re-zip
OUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
if OUT.exists():
    OUT.unlink()
with zipfile.ZipFile(OUT, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zout:
    for p in sorted(WORK.rglob("*")):
        if p.is_file():
            zout.write(p, p.relative_to(WORK).as_posix())
print(f"OK: wrote {OUT}")"}

arguments:
{"file_path": "/tmp/build_redline.py", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build a tracked-changes redline of the draft settlement agreement.

Preserves original formatting for unchanged paragraphs; for changed paragraphs,
keeps the bold lead-in label run and applies a word-level diff (diff_match_patch)
to the body, emitting native <w:ins>/<w:del> revision elements. New paragraphs
(new sections / carve-outs) are inserted as wholly-inserted paragraphs with an
inserted paragraph mark.
"""
import sys, zipfile, shutil
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree
from diff_match_patch import diff_match_patch

W = "http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main"
XML = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
NSMAP = {"w": W}
AUTHOR = "Greenleaf GC Review"
WHEN = "2025-05-16T00:00:00Z"

def q(tag): return f"{{{W}}}{tag}"

ORIG = Path("documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx")
WORK = Path("workdir_redline")
OUT = Path("output/settlement-agreement-markup.docx")

# ---------- style templates (clean, no namespace decls) ----------
HEADER_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:keepNext/><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="200" w:after="80"/><w:ind w:left="0"/></w:pPr>' % W
HEADER_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:b/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W
BODY_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="0" w:after="120"/><w:jc w:val="both"/></w:pPr>' % W
BODY_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W

class Rev:
    def __init__(self):
        self.n = 1000
    def next(self):
        self.n += 1
        return self.n
REV = Rev()

def parse_frag(xml):
    return etree.fromstring(xml)

def clone_rpr(rpr_el):
    if rpr_el is None:
        return parse_frag(BODY_RPR)
    return etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el))

def make_run(text, rpr_el):
    r = etree.SubElement(etree.Element(q("g")), q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return r

def make_ins(text, rpr_el):
    ins = etree.Element(q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr_el))
    return ins

def make_del(text, rpr_el):
    d = etree.Element(q("del"))
    d.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    d.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    d.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(d, q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("delText"))
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return d

def word_diff(old, new):
    dmp = diff_match_patch()
    diffs = dmp.diff_main(old, new)
    dmp.diff_cleanupSemantic(diffs)
    out = []
    for op, txt in diffs:
        if op == 0: out.append(("eq", txt))
        elif op == 1: out.append(("ins", txt))
        else: out.append(("del", txt))
    return out

def is_bold_run(r):
    rpr = r.find(q("rPr"))
    return rpr is not None and rpr.find(q("b")) is not None

def run_text(r):
    return "".join(t.text or "" for t in r.findall(q("t")))

def rebuild_paragraph(p_el, new_full_text):
    """Rebuild runs of p_el to turn old text -> new_full_text with tracked changes."""
    runs = p_el.findall(q("r"))
    old_full = "".join(run_text(r) for r in runs)
    rpr_body = clone_rpr(runs[-1].find(q("rPr")) if runs else None)
    # remove all existing runs
    for r in runs:
        p_el.remove(r)
    # Case: bold lead-in label run that is unchanged prefix of new text
    if runs and is_bold_run(runs[0]):
        label = run_text(runs[0])
        if new_full_text.startswith(label):
            # keep the bold label run as-is
            p_el.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(runs[0])))
            old_body = old_full[len(label):]
            new_body = new_full_text[len(label):]
            for op, txt in word_diff(old_body, new_body):
                if not txt: continue
                if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))
            return
    # Default: full-paragraph diff
    for op, txt in word_diff(old_full, new_full_text):
        if not txt: continue
        if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))

def make_inserted_paragraph(text, style):
    ppr_xml = HEADER_PPR if style == "header" else BODY_PPR
    rpr_xml = HEADER_RPR if style == "header" else BODY_RPR
    p = etree.Element(q("p"))
    ppr = etree.fromstring(ppr_xml)
    # inserted paragraph mark
    rpr_in_ppr = etree.SubElement(ppr, q("rPr"))
    ins_mark = etree.SubElement(rpr_in_ppr, q("ins"))
    ins_mark.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    ins_mark.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    ins_mark.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    p.append(ppr)
    ins = etree.SubElement(p, q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next()))
    ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR)
    ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(ins, q("r"))
    r.append(etree.fromstring(rpr_xml))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return p

# ================= REVISIONS (paragraph index -> new full text) =================

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build a tracked-changes redline of the draft settlement agreement.

Preserves original formatting for unchanged paragraphs; for changed paragraphs,
keeps the bold lead-in label run and applies a word-level diff (diff_match_patch)
to the body, emitting native <w:ins>/<w:del> revision elements. New paragraphs
(new sections / carve-outs) are inserted as wholly-inserted paragraphs with an
inserted paragraph mark.
"""
import sys, zipfile, shutil
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree
from diff_match_patch import diff_match_patch

W = "http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main"
XML = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
AUTHOR = "Greenleaf GC Review"
WHEN = "2025-05-16T00:00:00Z"

def q(tag): return f"{{{W}}}{tag}"

ORIG = Path("documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx")
WORK = Path("workdir_redline")
OUT = Path("output/settlement-agreement-markup.docx")

HEADER_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:keepNext/><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="200" w:after="80"/><w:ind w:left="0"/></w:pPr>' % W
HEADER_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:b/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W
BODY_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="0" w:after="120"/><w:jc w:val="both"/></w:pPr>' % W
BODY_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W

class Rev:
    def __init__(self): self.n = 1000
    def next(self):
        self.n += 1
        return self.n
REV = Rev()

def clone_rpr(rpr_el):
    if rpr_el is None:
        return etree.fromstring(BODY_RPR)
    return etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el))

def make_run(text, rpr_el):
    r = etree.Element(q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set("{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return r

def make_ins(text, rpr_el):
    ins = etree.Element(q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr_el))
    return ins

def make_del(text, rpr_el):
    d = etree.Element(q("del"))
    d.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); d.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); d.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(d, q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("delText"))
    t.set("{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return d

def word_diff(old, new):
    dmp = diff_match_patch()
    diffs = dmp.diff_main(old, new)
    dmp.diff_cleanupSemantic(diffs)
    out = []
    for op, txt in diffs:
        if op == 0: out.append(("eq", txt))
        elif op == 1: out.append(("ins", txt))
        else: out.append(("del", txt))
    return out

def is_bold_run(r):
    rpr = r.find(q("rPr"))
    return rpr is not None and rpr.find(q("b")) is not None

def run_text(r):
    return "".join(t.text or "" for t in r.findall(q("t")))

def rebuild_paragraph(p_el, new_full_text):
    runs = p_el.findall(q("r"))
    old_full = "".join(run_text(r) for r in runs)
    rpr_body = clone_rpr(runs[-1].find(q("rPr")) if runs else None)
    for r in runs:
        p_el.remove(r)
    if runs and is_bold_run(runs[0]):
        label = run_text(runs[0])
        if new_full_text.startswith(label):
            p_el.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(runs[0])))
            old_body = old_full[len(label):]
            new_body = new_full_text[len(label):]
            for op, txt in word_diff(old_body, new_body):
                if not txt: continue
                if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))
            return
    for op, txt in word_diff(old_full, new_full_text):
        if not txt: continue
        if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))

def make_inserted_paragraph(text, style):
    ppr_xml = HEADER_PPR if style == "header" else BODY_PPR
    rpr_xml = HEADER_RPR if style == "header" else BODY_RPR
    p = etree.Element(q("p"))
    ppr = etree.fromstring(ppr_xml)
    rpr_in_ppr = etree.SubElement(ppr, q("rPr"))
    ins_mark = etree.SubElement(rpr_in_ppr, q("ins"))
    ins_mark.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); ins_mark.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); ins_mark.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    p.append(ppr)
    ins = etree.SubElement(p, q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(ins, q("r"))
    r.append(etree.fromstring(rpr_xml))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set("{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return p

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build a tracked-changes redline of the draft settlement agreement.

Preserves original formatting for unchanged paragraphs; for changed paragraphs,
keeps the bold lead-in label run and applies a word-level diff (diff_match_patch)
to the body, emitting native <w:ins>/<w:del> revision elements. New paragraphs
(new sections / carve-outs) are inserted as wholly-inserted paragraphs with an
inserted paragraph mark.
"""
import sys, zipfile, shutil
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree
from diff_match_patch import diff_match_patch

W = "http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main"
XML = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
AUTHOR = "Greenleaf GC Review"
WHEN = "2025-05-16T00:00:00Z"

def q(tag): return f"{{{W}}}{tag}"

ORIG = Path("documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx")
WORK = Path("workdir_redline")
OUT = Path("output/settlement-agreement-markup.docx")

HEADER_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:keepNext/><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="200" w:after="80"/><w:ind w:left="0"/></w:pPr>' % W
HEADER_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:b/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W
BODY_PPR = '<w:pPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:spacing w:line="276" w:lineRule="auto" w:before="0" w:after="120"/><w:jc w:val="both"/></w:pPr>' % W
BODY_RPR = '<w:rPr xmlns:w="%s"><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:color w:val="000000"/><w:sz w:val="22"/></w:rPr>' % W

class Rev:
    def __init__(self): self.n = 1000
    def next(self):
        self.n += 1
        return self.n
REV = Rev()

def clone_rpr(rpr_el):
    if rpr_el is None:
        return etree.fromstring(BODY_RPR)
    return etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el))

def make_run(text, rpr_el):
    r = etree.Element(q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set("{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return r

def make_ins(text, rpr_el):
    ins = etree.Element(q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr_el))
    return ins

def make_del(text, rpr_el):
    d = etree.Element(q("del"))
    d.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); d.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); d.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(d, q("r"))
    if rpr_el is not None:
        r.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(rpr_el)))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("delText"))
    t.set("{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return d

def word_diff(old, new):
    dmp = diff_match_patch()
    diffs = dmp.diff_main(old, new)
    dmp.diff_cleanupSemantic(diffs)
    out = []
    for op, txt in diffs:
        if op == 0: out.append(("eq", txt))
        elif op == 1: out.append(("ins", txt))
        else: out.append(("del", txt))
    return out

def is_bold_run(r):
    rpr = r.find(q("rPr"))
    return rpr is not None and rpr.find(q("b")) is not None

def run_text(r):
    return "".join(t.text or "" for t in r.findall(q("t")))

def rebuild_paragraph(p_el, new_full_text):
    runs = p_el.findall(q("r"))
    old_full = "".join(run_text(r) for r in runs)
    rpr_body = clone_rpr(runs[-1].find(q("rPr")) if runs else None)
    for r in runs:
        p_el.remove(r)
    if runs and is_bold_run(runs[0]):
        label = run_text(runs[0])
        if new_full_text.startswith(label):
            p_el.append(etree.fromstring(etree.tostring(runs[0])))
            old_body = old_full[len(label):]
            new_body = new_full_text[len(label):]
            for op, txt in word_diff(old_body, new_body):
                if not txt: continue
                if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
                elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))
            return
    for op, txt in word_diff(old_full, new_full_text):
        if not txt: continue
        if op == "eq": p_el.append(make_run(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "ins": p_el.append(make_ins(txt, rpr_body))
        elif op == "del": p_el.append(make_del(txt, rpr_body))

def make_inserted_paragraph(text, style):
    ppr_xml = HEADER_PPR if style == "header" else BODY_PPR
    rpr_xml = HEADER_RPR if style == "header" else BODY_RPR
    p = etree.Element(q("p"))
    ppr = etree.fromstring(ppr_xml)
    rpr_in_ppr = etree.SubElement(ppr, q("rPr"))
    ins_mark = etree.SubElement(rpr_in_ppr, q("ins"))
    ins_mark.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); ins_mark.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); ins_mark.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    p.append(ppr)
    ins = etree.SubElement(p, q("ins"))
    ins.set(q("id"), str(REV.next())); ins.set(q("author"), AUTHOR); ins.set(q("date"), WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(ins, q("r"))
    r.append(etree.fromstring(rpr_xml))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, q("t"))
    t.set("{http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return p

    ("The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through good-faith negotiation between senior representatives. If the dispute is not resolved within thirty (30) days, either Party may submit the dispute to non-binding mediation administered by JAMS in Portland, Oregon. If the dispute remains unresolved after mediation, it shall be finally resolved by binding arbitration before a single arbitrator administered by JAMS in Portland, Oregon, under the JAMS Employment Arbitration Rules then in effect; provided that (a) Greenleaf may seek injunctive or other equitable relief relating to Sections 6, 7, 9, or 10 in any court of competent jurisdiction without first exhausting the foregoing procedures, and (b) nothing in this Section 20 applies to any charge, complaint, or proceeding before a governmental agency or to Delano's right to participate in any such proceeding. Judgment on any arbitration award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction. The arbitrator may award any remedy available at law or in equity and may award reasonable attorneys' fees and costs to the prevailing Party as permitted by law.", "body"),
    ("Section 21. Cooperation", "header"),
    ("Delano agrees to cooperate reasonably with Greenleaf in connection with any investigation, audit, claim, or proceeding (including the pending Oregon OSHA investigation referenced in the Recitals) relating to matters within the scope of Delano's former responsibilities, including by providing truthful information and, if reasonably requested, making himself available for interviews, depositions, or testimony; provided that (a) Greenleaf shall reimburse Delano for reasonable, documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in providing such cooperation, and (b) nothing in this Section 21 requires Delano to provide any information to, or refrain from cooperating with, any governmental agency, or to provide information that is not truthful. This Section 21 does not modify the release in Section 4 or the protections in Section 6.", "body"),
    ("Section 22. Insurance", "header"),
    ("Greenleaf represents that it has provided, or will promptly provide, notice of the claims resolved by this Agreement to its employment practices liability insurance carrier, Timberline Insurance Group (Policy No. TIG-EPL-2024-00391). Greenleaf's obligations under this Agreement are subject to Greenleaf obtaining any consent to settle required under such policy, and Greenleaf shall use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such consent. Greenleaf shall be responsible for any deductible, self-insured retention, or amount exceeding the applicable policy limits.", "body"),
    ("Section 23. Notices", "header"),
    ("All notices and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or sent by certified mail (return receipt requested) to the addresses set forth in the Preamble, or to such other address as a Party may designate by written notice. Notices to Delano may also be sent to Engel & Associates, P.C., 700 SW Taylor Street, Suite 400, Portland, OR 97205. Notices to Greenleaf shall be sent to the attention of the General Counsel.", "body"),
]

if WORK.exists():
    shutil.rmtree(WORK)
WORK.mkdir(parents=True)
with zipfile.ZipFile(ORIG) as z:
    z.extractall(WORK)

doc_xml = WORK / "word" / "document.xml"
tree = etree.parse(str(doc_xml))
root = tree.getroot()
body = root.find(q("body"))
paras = [c for c in body if c.tag == q("p")]
print(f"Found {len(paras)} paragraphs")

for idx, new_text in revisions.items():

for anchor_idx in sorted(insertions.keys(), reverse=True):
    anchor = paras[anchor_idx]
    for (text, style) in reversed(insertions[anchor_idx]):
        new_p = make_inserted_paragraph(text, style)
        anchor.addnext(new_p)
    print(f"  inserted {len(insertions[anchor_idx])} para(s) after {anchor_idx}")

tree.write(str(doc_xml), xml_declaration=True, encoding="UTF-8", standalone=True)

OUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
if OUT.exists():
    OUT.unlink()
with zipfile.ZipFile(OUT, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zout:

            zout.write(p, p.relative_to(WORK).as_posix())

result:
The Parties acknowledge and agree that Delano\'s employment with Greenleaf terminated effective March 14, 2025 (the \"Separation Date\"). Delano\'s last day physically present at Greenleaf\'s offices was February 10, 2025, the date on which Delano was placed on paid administrative leave. Delano confirms that he has received all wages and salary earned through the Separation Date, including payment for all accrued but unused paid time off (\"PTO\"). Delano acknowledges that his performance bonus for the 2024 calendar year had not been determined or paid as of the Separation Date, and agrees that any and all claims for unpaid bonus, commissions, or other compensation of any kind through the Separation Date are fully released and satisfied by the Settlement Payment provided in Section 3, and that no further wages, salary, commissions, bonuses, or benefits of any kind are owed to Delano by Greenleaf except as specifically provided in this Agreement. Delano\'s vested balance in the Greenleaf 401(k) plan, including all employer matching contributions that were vested as of the Separation Date, shall remain fully vested and non-forfeitable, and Greenleaf shall provide Delano with information regarding his 401(k) account balance and any outstanding loan balance so that Delano may roll over or distribute his vested balance in accordance with the terms of the 401(k) plan and applicable law. Greenleaf shall provide Delano with information regarding continuation of health insurance benefits under the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (\"COBRA\") within the timeframes required by applicable federal and state law. The Parties acknowledge that the terms of this Section 2 are not in dispute.

**Section 3. Settlement Consideration**

In consideration of the promises, covenants, releases, and agreements set forth herein, Greenleaf agrees to provide Delano with the following Settlement Payment:

**(a) Compensatory Damages Payment.** Greenleaf shall pay to Delano the sum of Three Hundred Twenty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$325,000.00) as compensatory damages for alleged emotional distress, reputational harm, and other non-wage losses sustained by Delano in connection with the claims resolved by this Agreement. This amount shall be treated as non-wage income and shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC issued to Delano. This payment shall not be subject to federal, state, or local payroll tax withholding, and Delano shall be solely responsible for the payment of any taxes due on this amount. Payment shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by wire transfer to an account designated by Delano in writing or, at Greenleaf\'s election, by company check delivered to Delano at the address set forth in the Preamble.

**(b) Back Pay.** Greenleaf shall pay to Delano the sum of Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars (\$75,000.00) as back pay representing lost wages from the Separation Date through the Effective Date, the unpaid performance bonus for the 2024 calendar year, and the value of lost employee benefits (including the employer 401(k) match) during that period. This amount shall be treated as wage income and shall be subject to all applicable federal, state, and local payroll tax withholdings, including without limitation FICA (Social Security and Medicare), federal income tax withholding, and Oregon state income tax withholding. Greenleaf shall report this payment on IRS Form W-2 for the applicable tax year. Payment of the net amount (after all applicable withholdings) shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by the same method described in Section 3(a).

**(c) Attorney Fees and Costs.** Greenleaf shall pay the sum of Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars (\$75,000.00) directly to Engel & Associates, P.C., 700 SW Taylor Street, Suite 400, Portland, OR 97205, as and for Delano\'s attorney fees and costs incurred in connection with the investigation, pursuit, and resolution of the claims resolved by this Agreement. This payment shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC issued to Engel & Associates, P.C., at its federal Tax Identification Number. Payment shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by wire transfer or company check delivered to Engel & Associates, P.C., at the address set forth above.

**(d) Accelerated Vesting of Restricted Stock Units.** Subject to the approval of the Compensation Committee of Greenleaf\'s Board of Directors pursuant to its authority under the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (the \"Plan\"), Greenleaf shall cause the immediate accelerated vesting of Five Thousand (5,000) of the Restricted Stock Units previously granted to Delano under the Plan pursuant to the RSU Grant Agreement dated July 1, 2022 (the \"RSU Grant\"), which RSUs were otherwise unvested and forfeited as of the Separation Date under the terms of the Plan. The Parties agree that for purposes of this Agreement, the fair market value of each accelerated RSU is Twelve Dollars and Fifty Cents (\$12.50) per share, based on the most recent independent 409A valuation conducted by Ridgepoint Valuation Services dated January 15, 2025, representing a total value of Sixty-Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$62,500.00). The remaining Two Thousand Five Hundred (2,500) unvested RSUs shall remain forfeited in accordance with the terms of the Plan and the RSU Grant. The accelerated RSUs shall vest and be delivered to Delano, or to a brokerage account designated by Delano in writing, within thirty (30) calendar days of the Effective Date. Any tax obligations arising from the accelerated vesting and delivery of the RSUs shall be governed by the terms of the Plan and applicable tax law.

**(e) Total Settlement Value.** The aggregate Settlement Payment under this Agreement is Five Hundred Thirty-Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$537,500.00), comprised of the compensatory damages payment (\$325,000.00), the back pay payment (\$75,000.00), the attorney fees payment (\$75,000.00), and the RSU acceleration (\$62,500.00).

**Section 4. General Release of Claims by Delano**

In consideration of the Settlement Payment and other promises set forth in this Agreement, Delano, for himself and on behalf of each of the Releasors, hereby irrevocably and unconditionally releases, acquits, and forever discharges each of the Releasees from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits, debts, dues, sums of money, accounts, reckonings, bonds, bills, covenants, contracts, controversies, agreements, promises, variances, trespasses, damages, judgments, extents, executions, losses, expenses, liabilities, and obligations of every kind and nature whatsoever, whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, accrued or unaccrued, fixed or contingent, that Delano has, has ever had, or may hereafter have against any of the Releasees, arising out of, related to, or in any way connected with Delano\'s employment with Greenleaf, the terms and conditions of that employment, or the termination thereof, from the beginning of time through the Effective Date.

Without limiting the generality of the foregoing release, Delano specifically releases and waives all claims arising under or related to any of the following federal, state, and local statutes, regulations, ordinances, and common law theories, as amended, to the fullest extent permitted by law:

> \(i\) Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, 42 U.S.C. § 2000e et seq.;
>
> \(ii\) the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 (\"ADEA\"), 29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq.;
>
> \(iii\) the Americans with Disabilities Act (\"ADA\"), 42 U.S.C. § 12101 et seq.;
>
> \(iv\) the Family and Medical Leave Act (\"FMLA\"), 29 U.S.C. § 2601 et seq.;
>
> \(v\) the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (\"ERISA\"), 29 U.S.C. § 1001 et seq.;
>
> \(vi\) the Equal Pay Act, 29 U.S.C. § 206(d);
>
> \(vii\) the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (\"GINA\"), 42 U.S.C. § 2000ff et seq.;
>
> \(viii\) 42 U.S.C. §§ 1981 and 1983;
>
> \(ix\) the Oregon Workplace Fairness Act and Oregon\'s employment discrimination statutes, ORS Chapter 659A;
>
> \(x\) Oregon whistleblower protection statutes, including ORS 654.062(5);
>
> \(xi\) Oregon wage and hour laws, including ORS Chapter 652 and ORS Chapter 653;
>
> \(xii\) any workers\' compensation claims under ORS Chapter 656 that Delano has actually filed and that have been accepted or denied by the insurer, to the extent such claims may be lawfully released and to the extent permitted by the requirements of the Oregon Workers\' Compensation Division;
>
> \(xiii\) any other federal, state, or local statute, regulation, ordinance, executive order, or constitutional provision relating to employment, employment discrimination, retaliation, wages, benefits, or conditions of employment; and
>
> \(xiv\) any and all claims arising under common law, including but not limited to breach of contract (express or implied), breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, tortious interference, defamation, slander, libel, fraud, fraudulent inducement, negligent misrepresentation, negligence, wrongful discharge, intentional infliction of emotional distress, negligent infliction of emotional distress, invasion of privacy, and promissory estoppel.

Delano acknowledges that he may hereafter discover facts different from or in addition to those that he currently knows or believes to be true with respect to the claims released herein, and Delano expressly agrees that this release shall remain effective in all respects notwithstanding any such discovery. Delano hereby expressly waives any and all rights he may have under any statute or common law principle that would otherwise limit the scope of this release to those claims actually known or suspected to exist at the time of execution of this Agreement.

Nothing in this Agreement, including the release in Section 4, prohibits or restricts Delano from filing a charge or complaint with, communicating with, participating in any investigation or proceeding conducted by, or cooperating with, any federal, state, or local governmental agency, including but not limited to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, the U.S. Department of Labor, Oregon OSHA, and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Nothing in this Agreement limits Delano\'s right to receive a monetary award, bounty, or similar payment from any such agency or under any whistleblower reward program, to the extent provided by law, and the release in Section 4 does not extend to any such right. Nothing in this Agreement requires Delano to notify Greenleaf of any communication with, or cooperation with, any governmental agency. The release in Section 4 extends only to Delano\'s right to recover monetary relief directly from Greenleaf or the Releasees.

**Section 5. ADEA / Age Discrimination Specific Release**

Delano acknowledges that he is knowingly and voluntarily waiving and releasing any and all rights or claims he may have under the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 (\"ADEA\"), as amended by the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act (\"OWBPA\"), 29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq. Delano further acknowledges and agrees as follows:

> \(a\) Delano has read this Agreement in its entirety, understands each of its terms and provisions, and agrees to be bound hereby.
>
> \(b\) The consideration provided to Delano under Section 3 of this Agreement exceeds anything of value to which Delano is already entitled and constitutes adequate and sufficient consideration for the release of ADEA claims contained herein.
>
> \(c\) Delano has been given a period of twenty-one (21) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement within which to consider whether to execute it, which Delano acknowledges is a sufficient and reasonable period of time. Delano is advised in writing to consult with an attorney of his choosing before executing this Agreement, and Delano has had the opportunity to do so.
>
> \(d\) Delano is entering into this Agreement of his own free will and volition, without coercion, duress, threat, or undue pressure from any person or entity, and with full knowledge of its terms, effects, and consequences.
>
> \(e\) Delano acknowledges that this Agreement is written in a manner calculated to be understood by him and that he, in fact, understands the terms, conditions, and effect of this Agreement.
>
> \(f\) Delano has not been coerced, threatened, or otherwise pressured into signing this Agreement, and Delano\'s decision to execute this Agreement has been made freely, knowingly, and voluntarily.

\(g\) Delano understands that, for a period of seven (7) calendar days following the date on which Delano signs this Agreement, Delano may revoke this Agreement and the release of ADEA claims contained herein. Any such revocation must be in writing and delivered to Greenleaf\'s General Counsel within the seven (7) day period. This Agreement and the release of ADEA claims shall not become effective or enforceable until the seven (7) day revocation period has expired without Delano having revoked this Agreement. If Delano revokes this Agreement within the seven (7) day period, this Agreement shall be null and void and of no force or effect.

**Section 6. Confidentiality**

Delano agrees that the terms, amount, and existence of this Agreement, including the Settlement Payment and each of its component parts, shall be kept strictly confidential. Delano shall not disclose, publish, or communicate any information regarding this Agreement, its terms, its financial components, or the negotiations leading to its execution to any person or entity, except as follows:

> \(i\) to Delano\'s spouse or domestic partner, provided that such individual agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the information;
>
> \(ii\) to Delano\'s legal counsel at Engel & Associates, P.C., or any successor legal counsel retained by Delano;
>
> \(iii\) to Delano\'s tax advisor, accountant, or financial advisor, solely for the purpose of obtaining tax, accounting, or financial advice, provided that such advisor agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the information; or
>
> \(iv\) as required by applicable law, regulation, court order, subpoena, or other compulsory legal process.

\(v\) to any federal, state, or local governmental agency, including but not limited to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, the U.S. Department of Labor, Oregon OSHA, and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; provided that, in such case, Delano may disclose only information necessary for such purpose and may not disclose the settlement amount or the terms of this Agreement except as required by the agency or by law.

In the event that Delano is compelled by legal process to disclose any information subject to this confidentiality provision, Delano shall provide Greenleaf with prompt written notice of such compulsion so as to permit Greenleaf to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. In the event that Delano breaches this confidentiality provision, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and all other available legal and equitable remedies, including recovery of damages sustained as a result of such breach.

Pursuant to the Defend Trade Secrets Act of 2016 (18 U.S.C. § 1833(b)), Delano is hereby notified that an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made (i) in confidence to a federal, state, or local government official or to an attorney, solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law, or (ii) in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding, if such filing is made under seal. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to conflict with 18 U.S.C. § 1833(b) or to create liability for disclosures of trade secrets that are expressly allowed by such statute.

result:
**Section 7. Non-Disparagement**

Neither Party shall, at any time after the Effective Date, make, publish, or communicate, or cause or encourage any other person or entity to make, publish, or communicate, any disparaging, defamatory, derogatory, or negative statements, whether written or oral, about the other Party, including but not limited to statements regarding the other Party\'s business practices, products, services, financial condition, management, employees, corporate governance, or the circumstances of Delano\'s employment with or separation from Greenleaf. For purposes of this Section 7, \"Greenleaf\" includes its officers, directors, and members of senior management, specifically including Sandra Wojcik (CEO), Tyler Huang (General Counsel), Patricia Engel (COO), and Leanne Foss (HR Director), each in their individual and official capacities. Any breach of this non-disparagement provision shall entitle the non-breaching Party to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies. The obligations set forth in this Section 7 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall continue for a period of three (3) years following the Effective Date. Nothing in this Section 7 shall prohibit either Party from making truthful statements to, or cooperating with, any governmental agency, or from providing truthful testimony in response to a subpoena or other compulsory legal process.

**Section 8. Tax Provisions**

The Parties acknowledge and agree that the tax treatment of the Settlement Payment shall be as follows:

> \(a\) The compensatory damages payment of \$325,000.00 described in Section 3(a) shall be treated as non-wage income, shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC issued to Delano, and shall not be subject to federal, state, or local payroll tax withholding. Delano shall be solely responsible for the timely payment of all federal, state, and local income taxes due on this amount.
>
> \(b\) The back pay payment of \$75,000.00 described in Section 3(b) shall be treated as wage income, shall be subject to all applicable federal, state, and local payroll tax withholdings (including FICA, federal income tax, and Oregon state income tax), and shall be reported on IRS Form W-2 for the applicable tax year.
>
> \(c\) The attorney fees payment of \$75,000.00 described in Section 3(c) shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC issued to Engel & Associates, P.C., at its federal Tax Identification Number.
>
> \(d\) The accelerated vesting of Restricted Stock Units described in Section 3(d) shall be treated in accordance with the terms of the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and applicable provisions of the Internal Revenue Code.

Greenleaf makes no representations or warranties to Delano regarding the tax consequences of any payment or benefit provided under this Agreement, and Delano acknowledges that he has not relied upon any statement or representation by Greenleaf or its attorneys concerning the tax treatment of any portion of the Settlement Payment. Greenleaf shall have no obligation to indemnify, defend, or hold harmless Delano for any tax liability, including interest and penalties, arising from or related to the payments or benefits made under this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for its own tax obligations arising from the transactions contemplated by this Agreement.

**Section 9. Non-Competition**

For a period of twelve (12) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant, independent contractor, officer, director, partner, member, owner, investor (other than as a holder of less than two percent (2%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly traded company), agent, advisor, or in any other capacity, engage in, be employed by, consult for, or otherwise provide services to any competitor in the organic food industry within the states of Oregon and Washington. This Section 9 is intended to comply with ORS 653.295 and shall be enforced only to the extent permitted by applicable law; if any portion is held void or unenforceable, including on the ground that Delano\'s employment was terminated by Greenleaf, it shall be reformed to the maximum extent permitted by law and the remaining portions shall remain in effect.

For purposes of this Section 9, a \"competitor\" shall mean any business, firm, company, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, or other entity that manufactures, distributes, markets, sells, or otherwise deals in organic food products, including but not limited to granola, trail mixes, dried fruit, frozen meals, plant-based snacks, organic beverages, or similar products.

In the event that Delano breaches this non-competition provision, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and all other available legal and equitable remedies, including recovery of damages sustained as a result of such breach. The non-competition period shall not be extended or tolled beyond the twelve (12) month period set forth in this Section 9, except to the extent expressly permitted by ORS 653.295.

**Section 10. Non-Solicitation**

For a period of eighteen (18) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether on his own behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity:

> \(a\) solicit, recruit, hire, engage, or encourage, or attempt to solicit, recruit, hire, engage, or encourage, any employee, independent contractor, or consultant of Greenleaf with whom Delano had material contact or about whom Delano obtained confidential information during the last two (2) years of his employment, to leave the Company\'s employ or engagement, or to accept employment or engagement with any other person or entity;
>
> \(b\) solicit, divert, or take away, or attempt to solicit, divert, or take away, the business or patronage of any customer, supplier, vendor, distributor, or business partner of Greenleaf with whom Delano had material contact or about whom Delano obtained confidential information during the last two (2) years of his employment with Greenleaf.

For the avoidance of doubt, this non-solicitation provision shall not prohibit Delano from making general solicitations of employment through public advertisements or employment postings not specifically directed at Greenleaf employees. In the event of a breach, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies.

**Section 11. Employment References**

Upon request from any prospective employer or other third party, Greenleaf agrees to provide a positive, neutral, or at minimum non-negative employment reference for Delano. Such reference shall confirm Delano\'s dates of employment (June 12, 2017, through March 14, 2025), his final title of Vice President of Supply Chain Operations, and shall include a statement that Delano was a valued member of the Greenleaf team who made significant contributions to the Company\'s supply chain operations during his tenure. All reference inquiries regarding Delano shall be directed to Leanne Foss, Human Resources Director, or her designee. Greenleaf shall instruct Sandra Wojcik, Tyler Huang, Patricia Engel, Leanne Foss, and all other current employees who may receive reference inquiries concerning Delano to refrain from providing any information beyond the reference described above and shall not, under any circumstances, provide any information suggesting that Delano was terminated for cause, for performance-related reasons, or in connection with any internal complaint or investigation; provided, however, that the foregoing limitations shall not apply to truthful responses required by applicable law, regulation, subpoena, or other compulsory legal process, or to truthful communications with a governmental agency in connection with an investigation or proceeding. Greenleaf shall issue written instructions to the foregoing individuals within seven (7) calendar days of the Effective Date implementing the requirements of this Section 11.

**Section 12. Mutual Release by Greenleaf**

Greenleaf, on behalf of itself and each of the Releasees, hereby irrevocably and unconditionally releases, acquits, and forever discharges Delano from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits, damages, losses, expenses, and liabilities of every kind and nature, whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, that Greenleaf has, has ever had, or may hereafter have against Delano, arising out of, related to, or in any way connected with Delano\'s employment with Greenleaf or the termination thereof, from the beginning of time through the Effective Date, except for claims arising from: (i) Delano\'s fraud, embezzlement, or criminal conduct discovered after the Effective Date; (ii) Delano\'s breach of any provision of this Agreement; or (iii) any obligation of Delano that survives termination of employment under any prior written agreement between the Parties, to the extent that such obligation is not superseded by this Agreement.

**Section 13. No Admission of Liability**

This Agreement is a compromise and settlement of disputed claims. Nothing contained in this Agreement, including the payment of the Settlement Payment, shall be construed as an admission of liability, wrongdoing, or violation of any federal, state, or local law, regulation, or ordinance by either Party. Greenleaf specifically denies that it engaged in any unlawful retaliation, age discrimination, or other wrongful or discriminatory conduct toward Delano or any other individual. Delano specifically denies any wrongdoing or misconduct in connection with his employment with Greenleaf. The Parties enter into this Agreement solely for the purpose of avoiding the costs, burdens, and uncertainties of litigation and to achieve a full and final resolution of all disputes between them.

**Section 14. Re-Employment Eligibility**

Delano shall not be barred from future employment with Greenleaf and shall be considered for open positions for which he is qualified, on the same basis as any other external applicant, without prejudice or adverse consideration arising from the circumstances of his separation from employment or the claims asserted in connection with this Agreement. Greenleaf shall not maintain any internal notation, flag, code, or designation in its human resources records, personnel files, applicant tracking systems, or any other employment-related database or record that would indicate Delano is ineligible for rehire or that would otherwise adversely affect Delano\'s candidacy for future employment with Greenleaf.

**Section 15. Representations and Warranties**

**(a)** Delano represents and warrants that:

> \(i\) He has not assigned, transferred, conveyed, or otherwise disposed of any claim, demand, right, or cause of action released herein to any person or entity;
>
> \(ii\) He has not filed any lawsuit, complaint, charge, or other proceeding against Greenleaf or any of the Releasees with any court, tribunal, or governmental agency, other than the Oregon OSHA complaint referenced in the Recitals above (Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872), and he agrees not to file any such lawsuit, complaint, charge, or proceeding in the future with respect to any claim released herein, except as may be required by law, to enforce the terms of this Agreement, or to file a charge or complaint with, or participate in an investigation or proceeding conducted by, any governmental agency; provided, further, that nothing in this Agreement limits Delano\'s right to receive a whistleblower award, bounty, or similar payment from a governmental agency to the extent provided by law;
>
> \(iii\) He has the full legal capacity and authority to execute this Agreement and to perform his obligations hereunder, and his execution and performance of this Agreement does not conflict with or violate any other agreement to which he is a party.

**(b)** Greenleaf represents and warrants that:

> \(i\) It has the full corporate power and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform its obligations hereunder, and the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action;
>
> \(ii\) The person executing this Agreement on behalf of Greenleaf is duly authorized to do so and to bind Greenleaf to the terms and conditions contained herein.

\(iii\) The accelerated vesting of Restricted Stock Units contemplated by Section 3(d) has been approved, or will be approved as a condition to the obligation to accelerate vesting, by the Compensation Committee of Greenleaf\'s Board of Directors in accordance with the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan.

**Section 16. Execution and Consideration Period**

Delano shall have twenty-one (21) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement to review, consider, and execute it. If Delano does not execute and return a signed copy of this Agreement to Engel & Associates, P.C., or to Greenleaf\'s General Counsel within the twenty-one (21) calendar day period, this offer of settlement shall automatically expire and be of no further force or effect, and neither Party shall have any obligation hereunder. Following Delano\'s execution of this Agreement, Delano shall have seven (7) calendar days to revoke this Agreement (the \"Revocation Period\"). Any revocation must be in writing and delivered to Greenleaf\'s General Counsel within the seven (7) day Revocation Period. This Agreement shall not become effective or enforceable, and no payments or benefits shall be due or payable, until the Revocation Period has expired without Delano having revoked this Agreement, at which time the \"Effective Date\" shall occur. If Delano revokes this Agreement within the Revocation Period, this Agreement shall be null and void and of no force or effect, and the Parties shall be restored to their respective positions as if this Agreement had never been executed.

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which, when taken together, shall constitute one and the same instrument. Facsimile signatures and electronic (PDF) signatures transmitted by email or other electronic means shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes of this Agreement and shall be sufficient to bind each Party hereto.

**Section 17. Severability**

If any provision of this Agreement, or the application thereof to any person, entity, or circumstance, is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect as if such invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision had not been included herein. The Parties agree that in the event any provision is determined to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving the Parties\' original intent to the greatest extent possible.

**Section 18. Entire Agreement**

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, representations, agreements, promises, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter, including but not limited to any prior offer letters, employment agreements, memoranda of understanding, or term sheets exchanged between the Parties in connection with the resolution of the disputes addressed herein. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and the RSU Grant Agreement dated July 1, 2022 shall remain in effect in accordance with their respective terms, except as expressly modified by Section 3(d) of this Agreement with respect to the accelerated vesting of the Five Thousand (5,000) RSUs and the forfeiture of the remaining unvested RSUs. No amendment, modification, supplement, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by both Parties. No waiver of any breach or default under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any subsequent breach or default.

**Section 19. Governing Law and Venue**

This Agreement, and all matters arising out of or relating to it, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Oregon, without regard to its conflict-of-laws provisions. The Parties irrevocably consent to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts located in Multnomah County, Oregon, for any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, except as otherwise provided in Section 20.

**Section 20. Dispute Resolution**
